diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Right ACA/MCA stroke with right ICA dissection
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with acute onset headache, disorientation, slurred speech, and left hemiplegia. Imaging studies, including MRI, indicated an acute infarct in the right frontal lobe and right basal ganglia involving ACA and MCA distributions. Additionally, a short segment dissection in the distal cervical course of the right internal carotid artery was identified, believed to be the source of the clot causing the stroke.
